Title: CdWRKY2-mediated sucrose biosynthesis and CBF-signalling pathways coordinately contribute to cold tolerance in bermudagrass

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a rate-limiting step of sucrose synthesis in the paper discussion
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native CdSPS1 promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by cold stress; only slightly activated by salt or drought stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in CdWRKY2-overexpressing bermudagrass hairy roots and reduced in CdWRKY2-silenced bermudagrass under normal and cold conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Compatible solute/osmoprotectant; supports photosynthetic recovery during cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress at 4°C; freezing treatment in Arabidopsis; salt and drought tested but weak effect on CdSPS1 expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases sucrose content and cold tolerance in transgenic plants; silencing decreases sucrose and cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis overexpression; bermudagrass VIGS knockdown; sucrose quantification; electrolyte leakage and OJIP/PSII measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CdSPS1 is the bermudagrass homolog of AtSPS2F and is positively regulated by CdWRKY2. 2. Overexpression of CdSPS1 in Arabidopsis increased freezing/cold tolerance and reduced electrolyte leakage under cold stress. 3. Silencing CdSPS1 in bermudagrass reduced sucrose content and increased cold sensitivity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve cold tolerance by enhancing sucrose biosynthesis; may be stacked with CdWRKY2 or CBF-pathway regulators
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth phenotype reported under normal conditions; possible negative feedback on excessive sucrose accumulation discussed
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in CdWRKY2-overexpressing Arabidopsis identified AtSPS2F induction; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes CdSPS1 module mainly functions in cold stress rather than salt or drought responses.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, group II, N-terminal WRKY domain with C2H2 zinc-finger motif
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Columbia ecotype; cold-resistant and cold-sensitive genotypes; commercial bermudagrass for hairy-root transformation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native CdWRKY2 promoter for GUS reporter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by cold; also induced by ABA, salt, and dehydration; higher basal and cold-induced expression in cold-resistant than cold-sensitive genotype
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in Arabidopsis and bermudagrass hairy roots; silenced in bermudagrass by VIGS
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold stress; ABA; salt; dehydration/osmotic stress
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sucrose biosynthesis and CBF-dependent cold signalling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation by binding W-box/core TGAC elements in target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CdSPS1 and CdCBF1 directly; activates AtSPS2F promoter in heterologous assay; indirectly promotes COR pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible negative feedback at sucrose level discussed, but no direct feedback mechanism established for CdWRKY2
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sucrose acts as osmotic protectant and supports photosynthesis during cold stress
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress at 4°C and freezing; ABA, NaCl, PEG/dehydration treatments
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive upstream regulator coordinating sucrose biosynthesis and CBF signalling for cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances sucrose accumulation and cold tolerance when overexpressed; silencing lowers sucrose and increases cold susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in Arabidopsis and bermudagrass hairy roots; VIGS silencing in bermudagrass; RNA-seq; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC; transient GUS; sucrose assays; physiological cold-tolerance assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CdWRKY2 is a cold-induced nuclear WRKY TF with higher expression in a cold-resistant bermudagrass genotype. 2. Overexpression enhances cold tolerance, while VIGS silencing impairs cold tolerance. 3. CdWRKY2 directly activates CdSPS1 and CdCBF1 promoters, thereby promoting sucrose biosynthesis and CBF signalling.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering cold-resistant bermudagrass via transgenic or editing approaches; may be combined with CdSPS1/CBF modules
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty under normal conditions reported; excess sucrose may trigger negative feedback
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT and CdWRKY2-overexpressing Arabidopsis seedlings before/after 6 h at 4°C; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CdWRKY2 also responds to salt, dehydration, and ABA, but CdSPS1 module appears mainly important for cold response.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: cold-resistant genotype; VIGS and hairy-root materials
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CdCBF1 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in CdWRKY2-overexpressing hairy roots and downregulated in CdWRKY2-silenced plants under both normal and cold conditions; induced by cold
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold stress; direct activation by CdWRKY2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: CBF-dependent cold signalling linked to stress-protective metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation by CdWRKY2 binding to its promoter; CdCBF1 then activates COR genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activated by CdWRKY2; positively associated with downstream COR expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important parallel target of CdWRKY2 contributing to cold tolerance through CBF signalling rather than direct nutrient-metabolite accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to CdWRKY2-mediated cold tolerance through activation of CBF-COR pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis in CdWRKY2 overexpression/silencing lines; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CdCBF1 is directly bound and activated by CdWRKY2. 2. CdCBF1 transcript increases strongly in CdWRKY2-overexpressing bermudagrass hairy roots and decreases in CdWRKY2-silenced bermudagrass. 3. CdCBF1 likely mediates the CBF-COR signalling arm of CdWRKY2-dependent cold tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving cold tolerance, potentially stacked with CdWRKY2 and CdSPS1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core direct target in Results, but it is not a nutrient-metabolism gene per se; retained because the paper frames it as a coordinated core pathway with sucrose biosynthesis.
